channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: El 60 % de las calificaciones en Harvard eran A. Ya fue suficiente
item.description: Nos merecemos la máxima calificación en esfuerzo.
item.dc:creator: Jason Furman and David Laibson

media:content.media:credit: Amani Willett para The New York Times

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Last ‘Late Show’ Features Famous Friends and Paul McCartney
item.description: Colbert was joined by famous friends, fellow hosts and special guests like Paul McCartney for the finale of “The Late Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Por qué tantos hombres están obsesionados con la testosterona
item.description: Desde el gobierno de Trump hasta los influentes de internet, la hormona se considera cada vez más la clave para alcanzar un nuevo ideal masculino.
item.dc:creator: Azeen Ghorayshi

item.title: 5 conclusiones de los demócratas sobre la derrota de Harris en 2024
item.description: Un informe preliminar publicado por el Comité Nacional Demócrata argumentó que Harris no se separó suficientemente del presidente Joe Biden
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: La derrota de la exvicepresidenta Kamala Harris en las elecciones presidenciales de 2024 fue el tema central de un informe preliminar publicado el jueves por el Comité Nacional Demócrata.

item.title: Picture Prompt: Graduation Day
item.description: Tell us a story, real or made up, that is inspired by this image.
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:credit: KT Kanazawich for The New York Times

item.title: In #MeToo Cases, Has ‘Believe Women’ Lost Its Power in Courtrooms?
item.description: The movement prompted a global reckoning about sexual harassment and sexual assault. But women taking the stand now are finding that defense teams are more aggressive.
item.dc:creator: Debra Kamin

media:content.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Rose McGowan, who accused Harvey Weinstein of rape, speaks to media outside the courthouse during his 2020 trial. The #MeToo movement has suffered losses since its height.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 22, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: ‘Ladies First’ Review: A Nightmare for Men
item.description: Sacha Baron Cohen and Rosamund Pike star in this role-reversal workplace comedy where women rule the world.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Kennedy

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘perceptive’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 56 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: ¿Deberías confiar tu salud a un quiropráctico?
item.description: Anuncian sus servicios para una amplia gama de afecciones, pero solo algunos de ellos están respaldados por evidencias.
item.dc:creator: Teddy Rosenbluth

item.title: Reseña de ‘The Mandalorian and Grogu’: una trama sin complicaciones
item.description: La película está estructurada de tal forma que bien podría haberse transmitido como dos episodios en una plataforma de transmisión en continuo.
item.dc:creator: Natalia Winkelman
item.category: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu (Movie)

item.title: Word of the Day: perceptive
item.description: This word has appeared in 56 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Stowaway Fox Gets Clean Bill of Health and a Name: Basil (Like ‘Dazzle’)
item.description: He made his way to New York on a ship from England and then found himself stuck in quarantine at the Bronx Zoo for months.
item.dc:creator: Ed Shanahan

media:content.media:description: A red fox that arrived in New York after hiding out on a ship from England spent the past several months in quarantine.

item.title: La ballena Timmy varó en Alemania y desató un drama nacional
item.description: Una ballena jorobada que quedó atrapada en el Báltico pareció unir a todo un país en esperanza. Luego vinieron los intentos de rescate y las acusaciones mutuas.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley and Jenny Gross

media:content.media:description: La ballena jorobada, apodada Timmy, atrapada cerca de la isla de Poel, Alemania, en abril.

item.title: Pope Leo Chooses Social Justice Over Pelvic Theology
item.description: The document will address A.I. and its impact on humanity. He’s also staying the course on Catholic social teaching.
item.dc:creator: David Gibson

item.title: Irán es liderado por un grupo de militares de línea dura
item.description: La toma de decisiones en el país está guiada por un grupo asociado al Cuerpo de la Guardia Revolucionaria Islámica.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una valla publicitaria en Teherán que muestra a los tres líderes supremos, el ayatolá Ruhollah Jomenei, el ayatolá Alí Jameneí y su hijo, Mojtaba Jamenei.

item.title: They Quarantined Together in London, Then Married in New York
item.description: After an early trip together turned into a quarantine during a record heat wave, Lila Newman and Lency Cantú blended New York and Tex-Mex traditions at their Hotel Chelsea wedding.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: Corrections: May 22, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, May 22, 2026.

item.title: After Years of Near-Misses, a Match That Felt ‘Like Divine Timing’
item.description: Marissa Anto and Kevin Anthony grew up near each other and went to school in the same city at the same time, yet it took Bumble to connect.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: Men, Hide Your Cats
item.description: Or at least keep them out of your online dating profile pics.
item.dc:creator: Michael Zadoorian

item.title: Thousands of Miles From the Iran War, Asia’s Currencies Feel the Strain
item.description: Soaring oil prices and a surging dollar are testing Asia’s foreign-exchange reserves, which were built up after the 1997 crisis.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson

media:content.media:description: A worker hauling frozen fish at a port in Pati, Indonesia. Rising diesel prices have left many boats docked.

item.title: She Was Covering President Biden. He Was Protecting Him. Love Took Flight.
item.description: Mariam Khan, a producer for ABC News, was in St. Croix covering the former president’s vacation when Phillip McCoy, a fighter pilot, spotted her on the beach.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: The Sweetest Ceremony — at Their Favorite Ice Cream Shop
item.description: Dr. Michelle Nguyen and Alex Dias got married among scoopers and freezers at Salt & Straw in Portland, Ore.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: Quote of the Day: ‘Era of Indifference’: Scarce Aid as Calamity Unfolds in Somalia
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, May 22, 2026.

item.title: In Jab at Trump, Mexico Targets Foreign Election Meddling
item.description: Mexico is moving to add protections from “foreign interference” in elections. The rules seem aimed at Washington, but they raised concerns about Mexico’s democracy.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas and Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

media:content.media:description: Voters casting ballots in Mexico City during Mexico’s general election in 2024.

item.title: 5 Notable Moments From Candace Owens’s Warm Interview With Hunter Biden
item.description: In a nearly two-hour conversation, Mr. Biden discussed his addiction to drugs and alcohol, and Ms. Owens apologized for her past remarks about him.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 22, 2026
item.description: Gene Louise De Vera opens our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dig in to this five-star themeless puzzle.

item.title: Iran and Oman in Talks Over Strait of Hormuz Ship Payment System
item.description: The discussions suggest that the United States and the Iranian government may not be close to reaching a deal to end a war that has badly damaged the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni, Vivian Nereim, Erika Solomon and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Ships and tankers in the Strait of Hormuz last month.

item.title: Cuba Celebrates Raúl Castro After the U.S. Accuses Him of Murder
item.description: Cuban officials closed ranks around Mr. Castro, their embattled former president, who was indicted on murder charges in the downing of two civilian planes 30 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles

media:content.media:description: Former Cuban President Raúl Castro, center, at May Day celebrations in Havana earlier this month.

item.title: Alberta Separation: What to Know Ahead of Oct. 19 Vote on Staying in Canada
item.description: Premier Danielle Smith announced that she would ask citizens to vote on whether they want to stay in Canada, or hold a referendum to secede.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff

media:content.media:credit: Amber Bracken for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Alberta’s premier, Danielle Smith, speaking during her regular weekly radio call-in show last year in Calgary.

item.title: Thousands Attend Prayer Service for San Diego Mosque Attack Victims
item.description: Thousands of people attended a funeral prayer service for the three men killed by gunmen at a mosque in San Diego on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna
item.category: Islamic Center of San Diego Shooting (2026)

media:content.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times

item.title: San Diego Mosque Shooter So Alarmed Police, They Seized Father’s Guns
item.description: In 2025, Caleb Vazquez’s obsession with mass shooters and Nazism prompted reports to the authorities. His father surrendered dozens of firearms.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín, Tim Arango and Chelsia Rose Marcius
item.category: Islamic Center of San Diego Shooting (2026)

media:content.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Families escorted their children across police lines after a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego on Monday.

item.title: Women’s Museum Bill Sinks Amid Dispute Over Trump and Trans Issues
item.description: The legislation to build a Smithsonian museum to honor women became contentious after it was amended to say the museum could only recognize “biological” females.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs

media:content.media:description: Efforts to advance a Smithsonian museum to celebrate the achievements of American women suffered a setback when a bill to pinpoint a location for it on the National Mall failed to pass in the House.

item.title: In San Diego, a Final Prayer Before Laying Heroes to Rest
item.description: Thousands of mourners filled a San Diego park to recite a funeral prayer for the three men killed by gunmen at a mosque on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín
item.category: Islamic Center of San Diego Shooting (2026)

media:content.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mourners gathered for a funeral prayer for three men who died on Monday while confronting two shooters at the Islamic Center of San Diego.

item.title: Trump Officials’ Strict Stand on Ebola Leaves Health Experts ‘Stunned’
item.description: Public health experts say the administration’s quarantine orders go beyond what is needed to prevent the U.S. spread of Ebola and hantavirus.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli and Jacey Fortin
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:description: Jay Bhattacharya, the acting director of the C.D.C., issued quarantine orders for two cruise passengers who wanted to leave the Nebraska facility and isolate in their home states.

item.title: In a Rarity, Republicans Stand Up to Trump
item.description: President Trump faced a wall of opposition from Senate G.O.P. lawmakers, in part over his plan to create a $1.8 billion fund to reward his allies.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Senator Susan Collins, Republican of Maine, is known to oppose the most extreme parts of President Trump’s agenda.

item.title: Outside Colbert’s Final Show, Fans From Near and Far Line Up for a Farewell
item.description: Hundreds of people, including a pope, staked out the Ed Sullivan Theater in Manhattan, hoping to get in, or just get a glimpse.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk, Alexandra E. Petri and Graham Dickie

media:content.media:description: The crowd outside the Ed Sullivan Theater on Thursday afternoon.

item.title: El Niño 2026: se esperan más huracanes en el Pacífico
item.description: El motor de este pronóstico es la casi certeza de que se formará un fenómeno meteorológico de El Niño este verano.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones

media:content.media:description: A satellite image showing Hurricane Melissa approaching Jamaica last October. Melissa was one of three Category 5 hurricanes last year.

item.title: Stream These 9 Movies and Shows Before They Leave Netflix in June
item.description: Among the highlights leaving for U.S. subscribers are the original “Sex and the City” and the only onscreen pairing of Harrison Ford and Liam Neeson.
item.dc:creator: Jason Bailey

item.title: Cuban Pilot in Florida Is a Defendant in Raúl Castro Indictment
item.description: The pilot, Luis González-Pardo, was one of the defendants in the indictment that included former Cuban President Raúl Castro.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams
item.category: Foundation for Human Rights in Cuba

media:content.media:description: Lt. Col Luis Raúl González-Pardo, left, and Lorenzo Alberto Pérez-Pérez, both MiG pilots in the Cuban Air Force, in an undated photo included in the federal indictment against former Cuban President Raúl Castro.

item.title: Trump to Deploy 5,000 Troops to Poland, Surprising the Pentagon
item.description: Defense Department officials had abruptly canceled the deployments of thousands of troops there just last week.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Chris Cameron
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Carolyn Van Houten for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump announced in a social media post on Thursday that the United States would deploy 5,000 troops to Poland.

item.title: GOP Pulls Measure on Trump’s War Powers in Iran, Lacking Votes to Defeat It
item.description: House Republican leaders abruptly scrapped a planned vote on a measure to direct President Trump to end the conflict or win authorization for it, amid party defections and absences.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro, Robert Jimison and Michael Gold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Exxon podría volver a Venezuela, poniendo fin a una larga confrontación con el país
item.description: El gigante petrolero de Estados Unidos está negociando un acuerdo para extraer petróleo venezolano después de décadas de disputas legales con el gobierno del país.
item.dc:creator: Anatoly Kurmanaev

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Un petrolero atracado en la refinería Cardón en Punto Fijo, Venezuela, en enero.

item.title: Judge Appears Hesitant to Order Work Stopped on Reflecting Pool
item.description: Judge Carl J. Nichols seemed skeptical that the changes President Trump has rushed to make would irreversibly deface the pool if he later found them illegal.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague and David A. Fahrenthold

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: This lawsuit argued in Thursday’s hearing is focused on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool’s new color, which Mr. Trump said he chose himself and calls “American flag blue.”

item.title: U.S. Invests $2 Billion and Takes Stake in Quantum Firms
item.description: The Trump administration said it had signed preliminary deals for equity stakes in nine quantum computing companies.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson

media:content.media:credit: Cindy Schultz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A GlobalFoundries facility in Malta, N.Y. The company would receive $375 million under agreements signed on Thursday by the Trump administration.

item.title: Valie Export, Who Made Art With Her Naked Body, Dies at 85
item.description: In provocative performance pieces and films, she forced viewers to confront misogyny in the media and society at large.
item.dc:creator: Will Heinrich

media:content.media:description: Ms. Export at the opening of a retrospective of her work at the Albertina in Vienna in 2023. What distinguished her art, beyond its aggression and sheer volume, was its lucidity.

item.title: Washington Is Still Trying to Rewrite Cuban History
item.description: How can Cubans move forward if they aren’t ready to reckon with their past?
item.dc:creator: Michael J. Bustamante

media:content.media:description: Images of Raúl Castro adorn walls around Havana, Cuba.

item.title: In Greenland, Protesters Denounce Opening of Bigger U.S. Consulate
item.description: “Go away!” demonstrators yelled outside the newly opened diplomatic outpost, as guests inside nibbled on musk ox hot dogs.
item.dc:creator: Maya Tekeli and Jeffrey Gettleman

media:content.media:credit: Oscar Scott Carl/Ritzau Scanpix, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Greenlanders protesting the new American Consulate in Nuuk, Greenland, on Thursday.

item.title: Senate Republicans Revolt Over Trump’s Fund
item.description: Also, Democrats release a draft report on what they did wrong in 2024. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: John Thune, the Senate majority leader, in the U.S. Capitol today.

item.title: Audit Immunity for Trump Family Puts I.R.S. in a Bind
item.description: Federal law prohibits the Internal Revenue Service from halting an audit at the direction of the president or his aides.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren

media:content.media:description: As part of an arrangement drawn up this week by Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, the Internal Revenue Service faces its most profound legal and ethical test in years.
